methodology.name: hegemon-formal-blueprint-dag-v1
methodology.summary: LeanMarathon-inspired formal-security blueprint: every release claim is a reviewed graph node with implementation bindings, call-order constraints, evidence, dependencies, and cheap falsification cases.

nodes.formal_statement: Every cargo-audit advisory or yanked crate that remains in release code or the formal-core checker lockfile is accepted only when the Lean dependency-audit policy preconditions hold: all waivers are well formed and unexpired, and every finding has an exact advisory/package/version/kind waiver match.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorem `Hegemon.Release.DependencyAuditPolicy.accepts_iff_dependency_audit_preconditions` pins the release waiver decision table, companion theorems pin exact-match acceptance and malformed/unwaived rejection precedence, generated vectors are checked by the policy helper, and `scripts/dependency-audit-gate.sh` remains the executable cargo-audit JSON and waiver-file evidence producer. The formal-core checker crate is audited separately so the assurance tooling does not bypass the same dependency discipline.

target_review.notes: The claim is deliberately operational: it proves review coverage for known dependency findings, not absence of future advisories.

falsification_cases.description: A cargo-audit finding with no matching waiver must fail the dependency gate.
The formal-core checker lockfile is audited independently from the root workspace lockfile.
Lean-generated dependency-audit policy vectors must fail on malformed waivers, exact-match drift, and unwaived findings.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node does not claim dependency freshness, advisory database completeness, or upstream advisory quality; it claims theorem-backed waiver accounting for known cargo-audit findings.

nodes.formal_statement: The release PQ-binary audit decision accepts exactly when source, dependency, and binary scans are all clean; any forbidden classical primitive hit in those scan classes rejects, with source findings taking precedence over dependency and binary findings.
nodes.informal_argument: The binary primitive audit is the release-facing check for the design rule that the native node must stay PQ-clean. The Lean theorem `Hegemon.Release.PqBinaryPolicy.accepts_iff_all_scans_clean` pins the release decision table, companion theorems pin rejection precedence, generated vectors are checked by the release audit helper, and `scripts/security-audit.sh` remains the executable scanner for actual source, dependency, and linked-binary evidence.

target_review.notes: The claim is scoped to the audited release node binary and the current forbidden-primitive pattern set.

falsification_cases.description: Forbidden ECC/pairing/RSA/trusted-setup symbols or dependencies must fail the security audit.
Lean-generated release PQ-binary policy vectors must match the release audit policy helper.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks the release audit decision table. It does not prove that grep/strings discover every possible primitive implementation or that every transitive crate is PQ-secure; those remain scanner-pattern and dependency-review obligations.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean transaction-balance transition, a valid balance witness must have concrete balance slots, the native slot delta must equal `fee - value_balance`, the stablecoin/non-native slot rules must hold, a non-stablecoin transaction has zero delta for every non-native asset, and a stablecoin transaction can have a nonzero non-native delta only for the explicitly selected stablecoin asset where the delta equals the authorized issuance amount. Under the explicit AcceptedProofArtifact balance-soundness assumption from proof acceptance to `validBalance`, the same conservation facts lift to accepted proof wrappers.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Transaction.validBalance_has_slots`, `Hegemon.Transaction.validBalance_rejects_slot_overflow`, `Hegemon.Transaction.validBalance_native_delta`, `Hegemon.Transaction.validBalance_stablecoin_rules`, `Hegemon.Transaction.validBalance_no_stablecoin_non_native_delta_zero`, `Hegemon.Transaction.validBalance_stablecoin_selected_delta`, `Hegemon.Transaction.validBalance_stablecoin_non_selected_non_native_delta_zero`, and `Hegemon.Transaction.validBalance_per_asset_transaction_conservation` prove core facts about the executable balance kernel and its no-asset-transmutation boundary as one top-level conservation theorem. `Hegemon.Transaction.AcceptedProofArtifact.accepted_proof_artifact_native_delta`, `Hegemon.Transaction.AcceptedProofArtifact.accepted_proof_artifact_no_stablecoin_non_native_delta_zero`, `Hegemon.Transaction.AcceptedProofArtifact.accepted_proof_artifact_stablecoin_selected_delta`, and `Hegemon.Transaction.AcceptedProofArtifact.accepted_proof_artifact_stablecoin_non_selected_non_native_delta_zero` lift those facts to accepted proof artifacts under the named proof-system balance-soundness assumption. `gen_transaction_vectors` emits Lean-derived native-fee, value-balance, non-native conservation, counterfeit rejection, stablecoin issuance, stablecoin mismatch, native-asset stablecoin rejection, and balance-slot overflow examples. The formal-core gate runs production `transaction-circuit` conformance against `TransactionWitness::balance_slots` and `TransactionWitness::validate`. The existing TLA+ model remains bounded abstraction evidence for the larger transaction balance/nullifier invariant shape.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for TransactionWitness balance-slot and balance-validation behavior, with the TLA+ model retained as bounded abstraction evidence.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the transaction-balance theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing production balance-slot or balance-validation behavior away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.
Removing `BalanceInvariant` or `NullifierUniqueness` from the model must fail the formal inventory check.
When local model checkers are installed, the wrapper can run TLC/Apalache against the transaction model.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks executable balance-slot and validation semantics, and conditionally lifts those facts to accepted proof wrappers under an explicit balance-soundness assumption. It does not prove note commitment hashing, Merkle hash-function security, nullifier derivation, ciphertext hashing, full AIR/proof-system soundness, or complete Rust refinement.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean Merkle-path transition, path folding uses position-bit orientation at every level, accepts the computed root when the sibling depth matches, and rejects mismatched depths or roots.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Transaction.foldPath_empty`, `Hegemon.Transaction.foldPath_even_step`, `Hegemon.Transaction.foldPath_odd_step`, `Hegemon.Transaction.verifyPath_accepts_computed_root`, `Hegemon.Transaction.verifyPath_rejects_wrong_length`, and `Hegemon.Transaction.verifyPath_rejects_wrong_root` prove the executable Merkle path control-flow facts. `gen_merkle_vectors` emits Lean-derived empty, left/right orientation, high-position, wrong-root, and wrong-length examples. The formal-core gate checks those vectors against the production `MerklePath::root_with` and `MerklePath::verify_with_depth_and_node` helpers, while `MerklePath::verify` delegates to the same fold helper with the real Poseidon2 `merkle_node` combiner.

target_review.notes: Accepted as Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for Merkle path fold, position-bit orientation, explicit-depth admission, and root equality semantics. Poseidon2 hash security/equivalence remains outside this claim.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the Merkle path orientation/depth/root theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing production Merkle path folding, depth admission, or root comparison away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.
Changing SmallWood native membership validation away from explicit MERKLE_TREE_DEPTH gating or MerklePath::verify must be caught by transaction-circuit regression coverage.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks Merkle path control flow only. It does not prove Poseidon2 merkle_node cryptographic security, Poseidon2 implementation equivalence, note commitment hashing, nullifier derivation, ciphertext hashing, full AIR/proof-system soundness, or complete Rust refinement.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean transaction public-input shape predicate, fixed-width vectors, boolean flags, inactive-field zeroing, active nullifier/commitment nonzero checks, indexed output-slot commitment/ciphertext-hash facts, nonempty transaction admission, canonical balance-slot asset ordering, and stablecoin asset membership must hold before verifier-facing public inputs are accepted.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Transaction.PublicInputs.validPublicInputShape_accepts_valid`, `Hegemon.Transaction.PublicInputs.validPublicInputShape_rejects_bad_input_flag`, `Hegemon.Transaction.PublicInputs.validPublicInputShape_rejects_inactive_input_nonzero`, `Hegemon.Transaction.PublicInputs.validPublicInputShape_rejects_active_input_zero`, `Hegemon.Transaction.PublicInputs.validPublicInputShape_rejects_inactive_output_nonzero_commitment`, `Hegemon.Transaction.PublicInputs.validPublicInputShape_rejects_empty_transaction`, `Hegemon.Transaction.PublicInputs.validPublicInputShape_rejects_bad_slot_zero`, `Hegemon.Transaction.PublicInputs.validPublicInputShape_rejects_padding_not_suffix`, `Hegemon.Transaction.PublicInputs.validPublicInputShape_rejects_duplicate_asset`, `Hegemon.Transaction.PublicInputs.validPublicInputShape_rejects_stablecoin_missing_asset`, and `Hegemon.Transaction.PublicInputs.validPublicInputShape_accepts_stablecoin_present` prove representative public-input shape acceptance and rejection facts over the executable predicate. `Hegemon.Transaction.PublicInputs.validPublicInputShape_output_slot_facts_at` and its helper theorems lift every indexed output slot to active/nonzero-commitment or inactive-zero-commitment-and-ciphertext-hash facts. `gen_public_input_vectors` emits Lean-derived malformed flag, inactive nonzero, active zero, empty transaction, balance-slot ordering, and stablecoin-membership examples. The formal-core gate checks those vectors against production `TransactionPublicInputsP3::validate` through the `transaction-circuit` test.

target_review.notes: Accepted as Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for the TransactionPublicInputsP3 verifier-admission shape. Statement-hash binding and proof-system soundness remain outside this claim.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the public-input shape theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing production TransactionPublicInputsP3 shape validation away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.
The release formal-core wrapper must generate public-input vectors and run the production conformance test.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks verifier-facing public-input shape admission only. Output-slot facts are public shape facts, not proof of recipient authorization, note plaintext correctness, encryption confidentiality, Poseidon2 implementation equivalence, note commitment hashing, nullifier derivation, ciphertext hash derivation, statement-hash preimage binding, STARK or SmallWood proof-system soundness, or complete Rust refinement. Statement-hash preimage binding is covered separately by formal.transaction-statement-hash-binding.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean transaction public/serialized input binding predicate, the public transaction fields and serialized STARK public-input fields must agree on merkle root, fee, signed value balance, balance-slot assets, and stablecoin binding payload before verifier-facing TransactionPublicInputsP3 construction succeeds.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Transaction.PublicInputBinding.bindPublicInputs_accepts_valid`, `Hegemon.Transaction.PublicInputBinding.validBinding_accepts_stablecoin`, `Hegemon.Transaction.PublicInputBinding.validBinding_accepts_omitted_balance_assets`, and the companion rejection theorems prove representative binding accept/reject facts over the executable predicate. `gen_public_input_binding_vectors` emits Lean-derived valid, omitted-balance-assets fallback, merkle-root mismatch, fee mismatch, signed value-balance mismatch, balance-slot asset mismatch/count mismatch, and stablecoin payload mismatch examples. The formal-core gate checks those vectors against production `transaction_public_inputs_p3_from_parts` through the `transaction-circuit` test.

target_review.notes: Accepted as Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for the public TransactionPublicInputs / SerializedStarkInputs binding seam. Statement-hash preimage binding is covered separately by formal.transaction-statement-hash-binding; proof-system soundness remains outside this claim.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the public-input binding theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing production public/serialized input binding away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.
The release formal-core wrapper must generate public-input binding vectors and run the production conformance test.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks public/serialized input field binding before verifier public-input construction only. It does not prove Poseidon2 or BLAKE3 implementation equivalence, transaction statement-hash preimage binding, note commitment hashing, nullifier derivation, ciphertext hash derivation, STARK or SmallWood proof-system soundness, or complete Rust refinement. Statement-hash preimage binding is covered separately by formal.transaction-statement-hash-binding.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean transaction proof-wrapper admission predicate, accepted wrappers must pass exact/canonical byte admission, supported backend routing, proof/public-input presence, verifier-facing public-input validity, top-level nullifier/commitment vector agreement with decoded public inputs, wrapper balance-slot agreement, and final verifier acceptance in that order. Accepted wrappers expose those statement-surface facts as a theorem-level conjunction for downstream proof-artifact lifts.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorem `Hegemon.Transaction.ProofWrapperAdmission.accepts_iff_proof_wrapper_preconditions` proves the executable predicate, and `Hegemon.Transaction.ProofWrapperAdmission.proofWrapperAccepts_implies_statement_surface` packages accepted exact/canonical byte admission, backend support, proof/public-input presence, verifier-facing public-input validity, nullifier/commitment vector agreement, balance-slot agreement, and verifier acceptance as theorem-level facts. Companion theorems pin valid acceptance, every rejection label, and the codec/backend/public-input/nullifier-vector/commitment-vector/balance/verifier precedence. `gen_proof_wrapper_admission_vectors` emits Lean-derived examples, and the formal-core gate checks them against `evaluate_transaction_proof_wrapper_admission`. Production Plonky3 and default SmallWood wrapper verification call the shared admission helper after decoded-field admission and around verifier acceptance, while the tx-proof manifest production summarizer exact-decodes nested proofs, reconstructs verifier public inputs, checks wrapper summary-vector agreement, and routes verifier rejection through the same admission helper.

target_review.notes: Accepted as Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for proof-wrapper admission ordering, including wrapper nullifier/commitment summary-vector agreement. Scope is admission over decoded predicates and production Plonky3, SmallWood, and manifest verifier result propagation, not proof-system soundness or a proof of bincode internals.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the proof-wrapper admission theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing production proof-wrapper admission away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.

The tx-proof manifest must reject nested transaction wrappers with trailing bytes, unsupported backend, missing proof bytes, missing serialized public inputs, invalid serialized public inputs, nullifier/commitment vector drift, or balance-slot drift.
The release formal-core wrapper must generate proof-wrapper admission vectors and run the production conformance test.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks proof-wrapper admission ordering over production-derived decoded predicates, wrapper summary-vector agreement, and verifier results. It does not prove bincode implementation correctness, STARK/SmallWood cryptographic soundness, verifier implementation correctness, statement-hash binding, or complete Rust refinement. Exact/canonical byte consumption remains enforced by production decoders and regressions.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean transaction spend-authorization relation, each indexed public input slot is either inactive with a zero public nullifier or active and authorized only by a private witness that reconstructs the note commitment, derives the spend-authority public key from the spend secret, derives the public nullifier from the same secret/rho/position tuple, and verifies Merkle membership under the stated root. Accepted proof wrappers imply this relation only under an explicit spend-authorization proof-system soundness assumption.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorem `Hegemon.Transaction.SpendAuthorization.authorizedInputWitness_implies_facts` exposes commitment, spend-authority, nullifier, and Merkle-membership facts for every authorized input witness. `Hegemon.Transaction.SpendAuthorization.authorizeInputSlots_head_active_facts` lifts those facts to the head active public input slot, while `Hegemon.Transaction.SpendAuthorization.authorizeInputSlots_active_input_facts_at` lifts them to any indexed active slot in aligned flag/nullifier/witness vectors. `Hegemon.Transaction.SpendAuthorization.authorizeInputSlots_input_slot_facts_at` covers every indexed slot, proving inactive slots carry zero nullifiers and active slots expose spend facts. `Hegemon.Transaction.SpendAuthorization.accepted_wrapper_implies_spend_authorization` plus `accepted_wrapper_implies_input_slot_facts_at` tie accepted proof-wrapper verification to total slot authorization through the named `SpendAuthorizationSoundnessAssumption`. Negative theorems reject wrong spend secrets, wrong public nullifiers, and wrong Merkle roots in the executable model.

target_review.notes: Accepted as a non-production spend-authorization relation and explicit soundness-boundary theorem. It does not prove that deployed STARK/AIR or SmallWood verification implies the relation.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the spend-authorization boundary theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
The release formal-core wrapper must include the spend-authorization module in the Lean root and claims ledger.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves the executable spend-authorization relation and its accepted-wrapper assumption boundary only. It uses abstract digest arithmetic and does not prove Poseidon2/BLAKE3 implementation equivalence, hash collision resistance, nullifier PRF security, note commitment hiding/binding, deployed STARK/AIR or SmallWood proof-system soundness, witness extraction, wallet key custody, note encryption correctness, or complete Rust/native-node refinement.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean accepted-transaction boundary, accepted proof-wrapper verification implies one abstract transaction relation containing statement-surface facts, valid balance slots and per-asset delta facts, public input shape validity, total indexed input-slot authorization facts, and total indexed output-slot facts, but only under explicit balance and spend proof-system soundness assumptions.
nodes.informal_argument: `Hegemon.Transaction.AcceptedTransactionSoundness.accepted_wrapper_implies_transaction_relation` combines accepted proof-wrapper admission with `BalanceSoundnessAssumption` and `SpendAuthorizationSoundnessAssumption`. The relation exposes accepted wrapper statement-surface facts, balance slot validity, public input shape validity, input slot authorization, and output slot validity. Companion theorems lift native and stablecoin per-asset deltas plus head active, arbitrary indexed active, total indexed input-slot facts, and total indexed output-slot facts out of that relation. This is the single Lean theorem boundary that future deployed STARK/AIR or SmallWood soundness work must discharge.

target_review.notes: Accepted as a non-production accepted-transaction soundness boundary. It is deliberately assumption-bearing and does not certify deployed proof-system soundness.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the accepted-transaction soundness boundary theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
The release formal-core wrapper must include the accepted-transaction soundness module in the Lean root and claims ledger.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves the abstract accepted-transaction relation and its assumption boundary only, including public output-slot shape facts. It does not prove deployed STARK/AIR or SmallWood soundness, witness extraction, verifier implementation correctness, cryptographic hash security, proof privacy, note encryption correctness, recipient ownership, plaintext-to-commitment correctness, or complete Rust/native-node refinement.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean canonical transaction verifier boundary, one canonical statement surface containing accepted wrapper admission, public/serialized input binding, statement preimage construction, proof binding-message construction, public nullifier/commitment/ciphertext vector agreement, indexed output-slot statement/binding facts, value-balance agreement, stablecoin payload agreement, active spend binding to the exact anchor/nullifier statement surface, field agreement, and balance-slot asset agreement is sufficient to derive the existing accepted transaction relation under one explicit deployed verifier soundness assumption.
nodes.informal_argument: `Hegemon.Transaction.CanonicalVerifierBoundary.deployed_soundness_implies_accepted_transaction_soundness_assumption` collapses the older independent balance and spend assumptions into `DeployedTxVerifierSoundnessAssumption` over `CanonicalTxStatementSurface`. `accepted_wrapper_and_canonical_statement_implies_transaction_relation` then reuses the accepted-transaction theorem, while companion theorems expose public binding validity, statement-preimage length, wrapper preconditions, public shape validity, shape/statement/binding vector agreement for nullifiers, commitments, and ciphertext hashes, output-flag/commitment/ciphertext-hash slot facts bound across public shape, statement preimage, and proof binding message, value-balance agreement, stablecoin policy/oracle/attestation/issuance payload agreement, active and total input-slot facts bound to the exact statement and binding nullifier vectors, native-delta conservation, and indexed input/output-slot facts from the same canonical surface.

target_review.notes: Accepted as a non-production canonical verifier-boundary refinement. It sharpens the accepted-transaction soundness assumption surface but does not certify deployed STARK, SmallWood, or native verifier soundness.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the canonical verifier-boundary theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
The release formal-core wrapper must include the canonical verifier-boundary module in the Lean root and claims ledger.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves an assumption-sharpening canonical statement boundary only, including modeled vector agreement and indexed output-slot facts across public shape, statement preimage, and proof binding message. It does not prove deployed STARK/AIR or SmallWood soundness, verifier implementation correctness, witness extraction, hash collision resistance, bincode/postcard implementation correctness, stablecoin policy/oracle validity, bridge mint authorization, note encryption correctness, recipient ownership, proof privacy, or complete Rust/native-node refinement.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean accepted-transaction boundary, every per-asset delta is authorized: native asset deltas match the modeled native balance, non-native unselected deltas are zero, and any nonzero non-native delta implies the selected stablecoin issuance/burn exception.
nodes.informal_argument: `Hegemon.Transaction.AssetIsolation.accepted_transaction_relation_authorized_asset_delta` lifts the accepted-transaction relation into one asset-delta policy. Companion theorems prove that unselected non-native assets have zero delta and that any nonzero non-native delta must be the selected stablecoin exception. Wrapper-level theorems compose the same result with `AcceptedTransactionSoundnessAssumption`, keeping the deployed verifier soundness gap explicit.

target_review.notes: Accepted as a non-production transaction-level asset-isolation boundary. It does not yet certify chain-level stablecoin or bridge mint authorization.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the asset-isolation boundary theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
The release formal-core wrapper must include the asset-isolation module in the Lean root and claims ledger.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves transaction-level asset isolation under the accepted transaction relation only. It does not prove deployed proof-system soundness, stablecoin policy/oracle/attestation validity, bridge mint authorization, external proof soundness, cryptographic hash security, proof privacy, note encryption correctness, or complete Rust/native-node refinement.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean transaction statement-hash, serialized public-input digest, and proof-binding grammars, transaction receipts, consensus tx-leaf binding, public-input digest records, and shielded proof binding hashes must commit to the same byte layouts: statement domain separator, merkle root, padded nullifier/commitment/ciphertext-hash lists, little-endian scalar fields, raw stablecoin flag byte, two's-complement signed i128 balances, tx-public-inputs-digest-v1 postcard bytes, and binding-hash-v3 length-tagged public partitions plus chunk-domain-separated BLAKE2 preimages.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Transaction.StatementHash.statementPreimage_accepts_valid`, `Hegemon.Transaction.StatementHash.statementPreimage_valid_length`, `Hegemon.Transaction.StatementHash.statementPreimage_accepts_padded_vectors`, `Hegemon.Transaction.StatementHash.statementPreimage_accepts_stablecoin`, `Hegemon.Transaction.ProofStatementBinding.bindingMessage_accepts_valid`, `Hegemon.Transaction.ProofStatementBinding.bindingMessage_accepts_stablecoin`, `Hegemon.Transaction.ProofStatementBinding.bindingMessage_distinguishes_field_padding_collision_from_padding`, `Hegemon.Transaction.ProofStatementBinding.bindingHashPreimage_valid_chunk0_length`, `Hegemon.Transaction.ProofStatementBinding.bindingHashPreimage_valid_chunk1_length`, and the companion rejection/separation theorems prove representative executable facts for the statement-hash and proof-binding preimage grammars. `gen_statement_hash_vectors` emits Lean-derived exact statement preimage bytes for full-width, padded, and stablecoin cases plus rejection examples, and emits exact serialized public-input digest preimage bytes for normal and stablecoin verifier inputs. `gen_proof_statement_binding_vectors` emits Lean-derived exact binding-hash-v3 message bytes, chunk-0/chunk-1 BLAKE2 preimage bytes, and a padding/sentinel collision case. The formal-core gate checks those vectors against the shared Rust `transaction_statement_preimage_from_parts` / `transaction_statement_hash_from_parts` helpers, `transaction_public_inputs_digest_preimage_from_serialized`, and the production `StarkVerifier::compute_binding_hash` transcript helper.

target_review.notes: Accepted as Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for statement-hash byte preimage binding, serialized public-input digest preimage bytes, and proof binding-hash-v3 transcript bytes. SuperNeo receipt paths are regression-checked against the shared statement-hash helper. BLAKE2/BLAKE3 cryptographic security, note/ciphertext derivation, and proof-system soundness remain outside this claim.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the statement-hash preimage theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.

Changing production statement-hash preimage byte order, padding, signed integer encoding, serialized public-input digest preimage, or digest input away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.
Changing consensus receipt or tx-leaf statement-hash binding away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.

Changing production binding-hash-v3 public partition lengths, scalar encodings, stablecoin encodings, padding-sentinel handling, chunk domain separation, or BLAKE2 preimage bytes away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.
The release formal-core wrapper must generate statement-hash and proof-binding vectors and run the production conformance tests.
Focused consensus tests must reject malformed inline transaction public-input vectors during receipt and claim derivation without panicking.
Focused SuperNeo receipt tests must reject malformed transaction public-input vectors during canonical and native receipt construction without panicking.
Focused SuperNeo receipt tests must compare canonical and native receipt statement hashes against the shared transaction statement-hash helper.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks the transaction statement-hash byte preimage grammar, serialized public-input digest preimage grammar, the proof binding-hash-v3 transcript grammar, and Rust digest calls over those checked preimages only. Blueprint bindings and regressions additionally require consensus and SuperNeo receipt derivation to use the fallible statement-hash path for untrusted inline artifacts. Proof binding-hash-v3 does not include transaction balance_tag, circuit_version, or crypto_suite; those are covered by the transaction statement-hash grammar. It does not prove BLAKE2/BLAKE3 collision resistance, Poseidon2 implementation equivalence, note commitment hashing, nullifier derivation, ciphertext hash derivation, STARK or SmallWood proof-system soundness, postcard implementation correctness beyond the checked vector cases, or complete Rust refinement.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean wallet note-ciphertext wire grammar, crypto-format note ciphertexts and chain-format fixed containers are accepted only when their byte fields are exactly consumed, the ML-KEM ciphertext length is fixed to 1568 bytes, chain container padding is zero, chain KEM length uses canonical SCALE compact encoding, accepted parser summaries bind the expected chain suite/KEM fields, and representative truncation, overrun, padding, noncanonical, and trailing-byte cases reject.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Wallet.NoteCiphertextWire.crypto_valid_accepts`, `Hegemon.Wallet.NoteCiphertextWire.crypto_truncated_after_kem_rejects`, `Hegemon.Wallet.NoteCiphertextWire.crypto_trailing_byte_rejects`, `Hegemon.Wallet.NoteCiphertextWire.chain_valid_accepts`, `Hegemon.Wallet.NoteCiphertextWire.chain_memo_overrun_rejects`, `Hegemon.Wallet.NoteCiphertextWire.chain_nonzero_padding_rejects`, `Hegemon.Wallet.NoteCiphertextWire.chain_noncanonical_compact_kem_length_rejects`, and `Hegemon.Wallet.NoteCiphertextWire.chain_trailing_byte_rejects` pin representative accept/reject facts over the executable parser grammar. `Hegemon.Wallet.NoteCiphertextWire.parsed_crypto_ciphertext_has_fixed_kem_len`, `Hegemon.Wallet.NoteCiphertextWire.parsed_chain_container_has_gamma_suite_and_fixed_kem`, and `Hegemon.Wallet.NoteCiphertextWire.parsed_chain_ciphertext_has_gamma_suite_and_fixed_kem` lift the sample grammar to generic accepted-summary suite/KEM facts. `gen_note_ciphertext_wire_vectors` emits the representative cases and the production wallet test checks them against `synthetic_crypto::note_encryption::NoteCiphertext::from_bytes` and `wallet::NoteCiphertext::from_chain_bytes`. Focused Rust regressions also check the no-panic truncated crypto parser and wallet chain parser canonicality rules.

target_review.notes: Accepted as Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for wallet note-ciphertext parser grammar. Privacy, note correctness, KEM/AEAD security, and hash security remain outside this node.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening note-ciphertext wire theorems or proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing production crypto or chain note-ciphertext parsing away from the Lean executable grammar must fail the generated-vector conformance step.
The synthetic-crypto parser must reject truncated KEM/payload prefixes without panicking and reject trailing bytes.
The wallet chain parser must reject memo overruns, nonzero fixed-container padding, noncanonical compact KEM lengths, and trailing bytes.
The release formal-core wrapper must generate note-ciphertext wire vectors and run the Rust conformance test.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks representative parser grammar for crypto note ciphertext bytes and wallet chain note ciphertext containers, plus generic accepted-summary suite/KEM facts for the modeled parsers. It does not prove ML-KEM security or implementation equivalence, AEAD confidentiality, wallet key custody, note plaintext or note commitment correctness, nullifier derivation, BLAKE3 ciphertext-hash security or implementation equivalence, DA availability, or complete wallet/native-node equivalence.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean privacy observer model, the public observer view of a shielded transaction consists of verifier-facing public inputs, ciphertext bytes, ciphertext summaries parsed from chain wire bytes, block height, and action index. Changing private witnesses or prover randomness cannot change that observer view, parsed summaries have the same length as the public chain ciphertext byte list, accepted parsed summaries have the expected chain suite/KEM shape, valid observer-chain surfaces tie parsed ciphertext count to the public active-output count, and equal public inputs, chain ciphertext wire bytes, and placement imply equal allowed leakage.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Privacy.Observer.observer_view_ignores_private_witness`, `Hegemon.Privacy.Observer.observer_view_ignores_prover_randomness`, and `Hegemon.Privacy.Observer.observer_view_ignores_private_witness_and_randomness` prove that note values, asset ids, note randomness, spend secret seeds, plaintext seeds, memo seeds, and prover randomness are outside the formal observer projection. `Hegemon.Privacy.Observer.parsed_chain_ciphertext_summaries_length` proves parser-derived summaries align with the public chain ciphertext byte list, while `Hegemon.Privacy.Observer.parsed_chain_ciphertext_summaries_have_chain_format` and `Hegemon.Privacy.Observer.observer_view_summaries_have_chain_format` prove accepted observer summaries have the expected chain suite/KEM shape. `Hegemon.Privacy.Observer.valid_observer_chain_surface_ciphertext_count` proves valid observer-chain surfaces have exactly one parsed ciphertext summary per active public output; `Hegemon.Privacy.Observer.same_allowed_leakage_preserves_active_output_count`, `Hegemon.Privacy.Observer.same_public_valid_observer_surfaces_ciphertext_count`, and `Hegemon.Privacy.Observer.same_allowed_leakage_of_valid_observer_chain_surfaces` lift that count boundary through same-public-input and same-leakage worlds. `Hegemon.Privacy.Observer.same_allowed_leakage_of_public_chain_wire_and_placement`, `Hegemon.Privacy.Observer.same_allowed_leakage_of_public_wire_and_placement`, and `Hegemon.Privacy.Observer.same_allowed_leakage_iff_observer_view_eq` define the equality boundary future ZK, ciphertext confidentiality, wallet metadata, and timing privacy games must refine.

target_review.notes: Accepted as a non-production leakage-boundary model. It defines the observer projection used by future privacy games but does not prove cryptographic zero-knowledge or ciphertext confidentiality.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the privacy observer boundary theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
The release formal-core wrapper must include the observer module in the Lean root and claims ledger.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves only the formal observer projection, parser-derived chain-wire summary boundary, accepted-summary suite/KEM format boundary, public active-output ciphertext-count boundary, and allowed-leakage equality boundary. It does not prove simulator-based zero-knowledge, proof-system privacy, ML-KEM/AEAD confidentiality, ciphertext indistinguishability, wallet metadata hygiene, timing privacy, network privacy, or complete wallet/native-node equivalence.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean nullifier-state transition, zero nullifiers cannot be staged or imported, successful staging prevents staging the same nullifier again, and successful import prevents both re-importing and re-staging the same nullifier.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Shielded.stage_rejects_zero`, `Hegemon.Shielded.import_rejects_zero`, `Hegemon.Shielded.stage_prevents_duplicate_pending`, `Hegemon.Shielded.import_prevents_reimport`, and `Hegemon.Shielded.import_prevents_restaging` prove the anti-double-spend state behavior over the executable nullifier kernel. `gen_shielded_vectors` emits Lean-derived nullifier examples, and the formal-core gate runs the production `protocol-shielded-pool` conformance test against them. The native node uses that shared helper for mempool staging, block validation, state replay, and author preview.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for protocol-shielded-pool nullifier stage/import behavior.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the nullifier-state theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing production nullifier stage/import behavior away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node does not prove full transaction balance conservation, note commitment correctness, or proof-system soundness.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean fork-choice transition, a candidate tip is selected over the current tip exactly when it has greater work, or equal work with greater height, or equal work and height with a lexicographically lower hash.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Consensus.higher_work_wins`, `Hegemon.Consensus.lower_work_loses`, `Hegemon.Consensus.equal_work_higher_height_wins`, `Hegemon.Consensus.equal_work_lower_height_loses`, `Hegemon.Consensus.equal_work_height_uses_hash_order`, and `Hegemon.Consensus.same_tip_not_better` prove deterministic two-tip ordering over the executable fork-choice kernel. `gen_consensus_vectors` emits Lean-derived fork-choice examples, and the formal-core gate runs the production `consensus::fork_choice` conformance test against them. `PowConsensus` and native block import both call that helper, and the blueprint now binds native announced-block fork-choice to the side-branch persistence or canonical reorg publication split. The existing TLA+ model remains the bounded aggregate-work/finality abstraction.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for the deterministic fork-choice ordering helper, with the TLA+ finality model retained as bounded abstraction evidence.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the fork-choice ordering theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing production fork-choice ordering away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.

When local model checkers are installed, the wrapper can run TLC/Apalache against the PoW model.
Native announced-block import must bind fork-choice comparison before either canonical reorg publication or noncanonical side-branch persistence can run.
falsification_cases.gate: cargo run --manifest-path scripts/hegemon_formal_core/Cargo.toml -- check-blueprint --claims config/formal-security-claims.json config/formal-security-blueprint.json

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean PoW admission transition, a block candidate is rejected when checked u64 next-height, pow_bits, timestamp, compact target, hash threshold, claimed cumulative Work48, or Work48 overflow violate the specified arithmetic policy; otherwise the expected cumulative work is accepted.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Consensus.compactTarget_rejects_zero_mantissa`, `Hegemon.Consensus.compactTarget_rejects_shifted_zero_target`, `Hegemon.Consensus.compactTarget_rejects_large_exponent`, `Hegemon.Consensus.checkedNextU64_rejects_max`, `Hegemon.Consensus.checkedNextU64_accepts_predecessor`, `Hegemon.Consensus.timestamp_rejects_parent_equal`, `Hegemon.Consensus.timestamp_rejects_median_equal`, `Hegemon.Consensus.timestamp_rejects_future_skew`, `Hegemon.Consensus.checkedWorkAdd_ok`, `Hegemon.Consensus.checkedWorkAdd_rejects_overflow`, `Hegemon.Consensus.powAdmission_rejects_height`, `Hegemon.Consensus.powAdmission_rejects_height_overflow`, `Hegemon.Consensus.powAdmission_rejects_pow_bits`, `Hegemon.Consensus.powAdmission_rejects_insufficient_work`, and `Hegemon.Consensus.powAdmission_accepts_valid` prove core facts about the executable PoW admission table. `gen_pow_vectors` emits Lean-derived examples, and the formal-core gate checks them against the production `evaluate_pow_admission` helper used by `PowConsensus` plus the `consensus-light-client` compact-target, hash-threshold, and Work48 cumulative-work helpers used by native import and bridge verification. The blueprint now requires `PowConsensus::apply_block` to propagate `evaluate_pow_admission` before proof verification or node insertion.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for PoW checked next-height, target, timestamp, hash-threshold, and Work48 cumulative-work admission semantics.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for PoW miner identity and signature admission ordering. ML-DSA cryptographic security and implementation correctness remain outside this node.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean action-scope admission transition, a native action is routed to bridge, candidate-artifact, coinbase, or shielded-transfer validation only when candidate-artifact payload scoping, route-specific state-delta scope, candidate payload presence, and transfer public metadata shape predicates satisfy the route's preconditions; rejection precedence is fixed by the executable table.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorem `Hegemon.Native.ActionScopeAdmission.accepts_iff_scope_preconditions` proves the complete executable route/scope predicate, with companion theorems for all rejection routes and wrong-candidate-artifact-route precedence. `gen_action_scope_admission_vectors` emits Lean-derived route and reject examples. The formal-core gate checks those vectors against the production `evaluate_native_action_scope_admission` helper now called by native mempool and block action validation. Native regressions reject candidate artifacts on the wrong route, bridge/candidate/coinbase state-delta smuggling, missing candidate payloads, unsupported transfer routes, and malformed transfer public metadata.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for native action route/scope admission. Scope is the cheap route and public metadata-shape table before deep action payload validation.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean decoded bridge-payload admission transition, native bridge action validation accepts only when the action is in the bridge family, carries no shielded state deltas or candidate artifact payload, uses a supported bridge action kind, carries a nonempty outbound payload for outbound messages, and for inbound messages carries a nonempty proof receipt, binds the replay key to the embedded message source/nonce, targets the Hegemon chain id, and binds the embedded payload to its payload hash; rejection precedence is fixed by the executable table. The bridge mint-safety lift proves that an accepted inbound payload plus a fresh replay import consumes the replay key once and makes immediate reimport reject, and that accepted block/ledger replay couples consumed bridge replay-key uniqueness with the claimed native supply digest.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorem `Hegemon.Native.BridgeActionPayloadAdmission.accepts_iff_payload_preconditions` proves the complete executable decoded bridge payload-admission predicate, with companion theorems for valid outbound/inbound/register payloads, non-bridge action rejection, state-delta smuggling rejection, unsupported action rejection, outbound empty-payload rejection, inbound empty-proof rejection, inbound replay-key mismatch rejection, inbound destination mismatch rejection, and inbound payload-hash mismatch rejection. `Hegemon.Native.BridgeMintSafety.accepted_inbound_payload_fresh_replay_mint_safe` packages the accepted inbound authorization facts with fresh replay import so the key is consumed once and cannot be reimported; `Hegemon.Native.BridgeMintSafety.accepted_block_replay_couples_bridge_replay_and_supply` and `Hegemon.Native.BridgeMintSafety.accepted_native_ledger_bridge_replay_supply_coupling` lift that replay/supply coupling through accepted block and ledger replay. `gen_bridge_action_payload_admission_vectors` emits Lean-derived accept/reject examples. The formal-core gate checks those vectors against the production `evaluate_native_bridge_action_payload_admission` helper now called by native bridge action validation before the disabled RISC Zero receipt verifier path. Native regressions reject empty outbound payloads and inbound proof/replay/destination/payload-hash mismatches before receipt verification.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for decoded bridge action payload admission. nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean native bridge-witness latest-message backscan, the no-explicit-hash `hegemon_exportBridgeWitness` path scans a bounded newest-to-oldest canonical-height window, skips missing height indexes and missing block records, fails closed on canonical block-action decode failure, selects the first eligible block whose bridge-message list contains the requested message index, and rejects if the bounded scan has no eligible bridge message.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean recursive evaluator `Hegemon.Native.BridgeWitnessBackscan.evaluateBridgeWitnessBackscan` models the scan entries in descending order. General theorems prove selectable heads are selected, missing hash and missing block records skip to the tail, decode failure precedes any tail match, message-index misses skip to the tail, and an empty scan rejects. Concrete theorems pin newest-candidate selection, skipped-record behavior, decode-failure-before-older-match behavior, and no-eligible-message rejection. `gen_bridge_witness_backscan_vectors` emits Lean-derived examples, and the formal-core gate checks them against the production `evaluate_native_bridge_witness_backscan` helper now called by `latest_bridge_message_block_hash`; a focused regression corrupts a newer canonical block and verifies latest witness export fails closed instead of silently selecting an older bridge message.

target_review.notes: Accepted as Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for bounded latest-message bridge witness backscan selection. Scope is deterministic scan selection/rejection before the resolved block's export-admission gate.

Companion theorems pin valid mixed blocks, non-transfer interleaving, action hash mismatch, duplicate hashes, scope-before-payload, bridge payload-before-replay, prior and cross-action bridge replay duplicates, transfer payload-before-order, descending transfer order, transfer order-before-state, and transfer-state rejection behavior. `gen_block_action_validation_vectors` emits Lean-derived examples checked against production helper functions introduced in `node/src/native/mod.rs`, and `validate_block_actions_locked` now routes decoded block validation through that helper before preserving the existing user-facing rejection errors. nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorem `Hegemon.Native.BlockCommitmentAdmission.accepts_iff_commitment_preconditions` proves the complete executable admission predicate, with companion theorems for every field mismatch and state-root-before-supply-digest precedence. `gen_block_commitment_admission_vectors` emits Lean-derived accept/reject examples. The formal-core gate checks those vectors against the production `evaluate_native_block_commitment_admission` helper now called by native mined-block, announced-block, and replay admission. Local mined-block import must first recompute and propagate checked native supply advancement so the work template's claimed supply digest can feed the commitment table; the direct commitment precheck is then required to fail closed before replay refinement, PoW verification, artifact validation, or atomic commit. Native regressions also construct internally consistent PoW headers with counterfeit body commitments and verify import/replay rejection.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean action-stream effect transition over an ordered list of decoded native action summaries, acceptance records the commitment-start index for each accepted action, advances the leaf count with checked u64 arithmetic, imports nullifiers into a shared stream state so prior-spent, intra-action, and cross-action duplicates reject, preserves `List.Nodup` for the accumulated imported-nullifier state when the starting state is `List.Nodup`, imports inbound replay keys into a shared stream state so prior-consumed and cross-action replay duplicates reject, preserves `List.Nodup` for the accumulated consumed bridge replay state when the starting state is `List.Nodup`, and follows the production rejection precedence of count mismatch, commitment-index overflow, nullifier failure, then bridge replay duplicate.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorem `Hegemon.Native.ActionStreamEffect.accepts_iff_stream_preconditions` pins the executable stream predicate, while `valid_two_action_stream_accepts`, `cross_action_duplicate_nullifier_rejects`, `within_action_duplicate_nullifier_rejects`, `prior_spent_duplicate_nullifier_rejects`, `zero_nullifier_second_action_rejects`, `cross_action_bridge_replay_duplicate_rejects`, `prior_consumed_bridge_replay_duplicate_rejects`, `second_action_commitment_overflow_rejects`, and the companion precedence theorems prove representative ordered-stream cases. `evaluateActionStreamEffect_preserves_imported_nullifier_nodup` proves the generic imported-nullifier `Nodup` preservation used by the accepted-chain lift, while `evaluateActionStreamEffect_preserves_imported_bridge_replay_nodup` proves the matching consumed-bridge-replay `Nodup` preservation used by the accepted ledger-chain lift. `gen_action_stream_effect_vectors` emits Lean-derived examples checked against `evaluate_native_action_stream_effect`, the production helper now called by native memory replay, pending-action planning, and canonical-index rebuild before commitment/nullifier/replay state mutation or sled writes.

target_review.notes: Accepted as Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for ordered native action-stream planning. It narrows the multi-action replay gap; scope excludes raw action decoding, cryptographic derivations, storage durability, and full native-node equivalence.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean block replay-refinement transition, native replay/accounting acceptance implies the ordered action-stream effect table accepted, checked native supply advancement produced the claimed supply digest, and every block commitment-admission precondition holds. The accepted-chain lift threads parent supply, commitment leaf cursor, action-stream imported nullifier state, and imported bridge replay state across a list of accepted native replay blocks, proving that accepted ledger replay equals checked native supply advancement, carries the same leaf cursor as accepted block summaries, has canonical commitment-start plans for every accepted block, preserves final nullifier uniqueness, and preserves final consumed bridge replay-key uniqueness. `accepted_native_ledger_replay_chain_startup_equivalence` packages those five startup/replay facts into one theorem. The traced Lean transition has the same result as the untraced transition, and accepted replay has the canonical stage trace action-stream, supply, block-commitment, accepted. In particular, an accepted multi-action replay summary cannot carry a counterfeit state-root predicate, claimed supply, stale leaf cursor, stale spent-nullifier state, or stale consumed-bridge-replay state under the modeled production-derived predicates.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorem `Hegemon.Native.BlockReplayRefinement.accepts_iff_block_replay_preconditions` proves the composed executable predicate over ordered action-stream planning, native supply advancement, and block commitment admission. `accepted_claims_expected_supply`, `accepted_has_action_stream_effect`, `accepted_implies_commitment_preconditions`, and `accepted_forbids_counterfeit_state_root` extract the facts needed by the per-block no-counterfeiting boundary. The original accepted-chain theorems `accepted_native_replay_chain_no_counterfeiting`, `accepted_native_replay_chain_nullifier_preconditions`, `accepted_native_replay_chain_nullifiers_unique`, and `accepted_native_replay_chain_startup_equivalence` retain the supply/nullifier replay surface. The stronger ledger-chain theorems `accepted_native_ledger_replay_chain_supply_from`, `accepted_native_ledger_replay_chain_leaf_cursor_from`, `accepted_native_ledger_replay_chain_commitment_plans_canonical_from`, `accepted_native_ledger_replay_chain_nullifiers_unique_from`, `accepted_native_ledger_replay_chain_bridge_replays_unique_from`, and `accepted_native_ledger_replay_chain_startup_equivalence` lift that boundary across blocks while carrying supply, leaf cursor, spent nullifiers, and consumed bridge replay keys. `stale_leaf_native_ledger_replay_chain_rejects`, `duplicate_bridge_replay_native_ledger_replay_chain_rejects`, and `stale_bridge_replay_native_ledger_replay_chain_rejects` pin representative chain-level carried-state rejections. `traced_result_matches_untraced` proves that the traced evaluator used for conformance has the same result as the executable transition, and `accepted_trace_is_canonical` pins the accepted-stage order. `valid_two_action_replay_accepts`, `duplicate_nullifier_replay_rejects`, and `cross_action_bridge_replay_duplicate_rejects` pin representative multi-action replay behavior. `gen_block_replay_refinement_vectors` emits Lean-derived examples checked against the `node/src/native/mod.rs` production helper used by mined-block import, announced-block import, and canonical replay; that helper now materializes inline/sidecar ciphertext bytes before constructing the action-stream steps, passes materialized ciphertext counts to the production ordered stream helper, and composes block-commitment plus checked native supply advancement over height, fee total, and coinbase presence while exposing the production stage trace for differential checking.

target_review.notes: Accepted as a compositional Lean refinement boundary over existing production-derived predicates. It narrows the gap toward end-to-end no-counterfeiting but is not a complete native-node equivalence proof.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the block replay-refinement theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.

Changing the Rust composite replay/accounting helper used by mined-block import, announced-block import, and canonical replay away from the Lean executable spec or its staged transition trace must fail the generated-vector conformance step.

Changing the production replay helper stage order without changing the final result must fail because Rust compares its production trace to Lean-generated `expected_trace` for every replay-refinement vector.
The release formal-core wrapper must generate block replay-refinement vectors and run the Rust conformance test over the production helper composition.
The blueprint gate must reject the block replay-refinement claim if mined-block import, announced-block import, or canonical replay stop calling the non-test production replay helper.
falsification_cases.gate: cargo run --quiet --manifest-path scripts/hegemon_formal_core/Cargo.toml -- check-blueprint config/formal-security-blueprint.json --claims config/formal-security-claims.json

falsification_cases.description: The blueprint gate must reject the block replay-refinement claim if the replay helper no longer precedes the declared payload validation, state mutation, persistence, broadcast, or explicit-chain reorganization callees in the critical import/replay callers.
falsification_cases.gate: cargo run --quiet --manifest-path scripts/hegemon_formal_core/Cargo.toml -- check-blueprint config/formal-security-blueprint.json --claims config/formal-security-claims.json

falsification_cases.description: Native preview, memory replay, and block replay-refinement must reject sidecar actions whose ciphertext payloads are not materialized from canonical DA storage instead of self-fulfilling ciphertext counts from commitments.
Announced-block import must reject a forged body commitment through the replay-refinement gate before malformed action payload validation can mask the no-counterfeiting failure.
A nonwinning announced side branch must persist as a hash-addressed block record across reopen without replacing the canonical height index.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks the composed replay/accounting helper now called by native mined-block import, announced-block import, and explicit-chain canonical branch replay over ordered decoded action summaries, materialized inline/sidecar ciphertext counts, checked native supply advancement, and block commitment predicates. The blueprint/regression gate now also binds nonwinning announced side-branch persistence through a named block-record helper and checks reload without canonical-height replacement. It does not prove SCALE decoder correctness, sled transaction implementation correctness, filesystem/fsync durability, commitment-tree/hash security, BLAKE3/Blake2 implementation equivalence, full raw-action native-node replay equivalence, tx-leaf proof soundness, recursive proof soundness, PoW threshold validity, or complete native-node equivalence.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean message-root transcript function, valid 48-byte message hashes are encoded as domain || len(count-bytes) || count || len(hash) || hash..., invalid hash lengths are rejected before the Rust root helper can be called, and ordered pairs produce different transcripts when reversed.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean `Hegemon.Bridge.MessageRoot` kernel fixes the byte grammar that feeds bridge `message_root` hashing. `gen_bridge_vectors` emits empty, single, ordered-pair, reversed-pair, and invalid hash-length cases, and the production `protocol-kernel` conformance test checks the Rust root preimage helper against those Lean bytes. The independent bridge reference vectors continue to rederive the BLAKE3 XOF-48 payload hash, canonical message hash, single-message root, and inbound replay key without importing production bridge helpers.

target_review.notes: The target is accepted for Lean-backed transcript grammar and independent BLAKE3 reference vectors. Larger bridge light-client and external-chain proof systems remain out of scope.

falsification_cases.description: Changing the Rust bridge message-root transcript domain, count bytes, length prefixes, or ordered hash sequence must fail the Lean-generated conformance test.
Changing payload bytes or expected payload hash must fail vector verification.
Replay keys must differ across source chain ids and nonces.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node covers bridge message-root transcript grammar, canonical vector derivations, and replay-key vector checks. It does not prove BLAKE3 cryptographic security or implementation equivalence, every bridge light-client proof system, or external-chain covenant behavior.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean replay-state transition, successful staging prevents staging the same key again, and successful import prevents both re-importing and re-staging the same key.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Bridge.stage_prevents_duplicate_pending`, `Hegemon.Bridge.import_prevents_reimport`, and `Hegemon.Bridge.import_prevents_restaging` prove the no-duplicate properties over the executable replay-state kernel. `gen_bridge_vectors` emits Lean-derived bridge encoding and replay examples, and the formal-core gate runs the production `protocol-kernel` conformance test against them. Persisted native startup reload of consumed replay keys is separately theorem-backed by `native.bridge-replay-reload`.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for protocol-kernel bridge encoding and replay helper behavior. Native persisted replay reload canonical binding is separately covered by native.bridge-replay-reload.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the replay-state theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing production bridge encoding or replay helper behavior away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.
The production native startup reload claim must remain present for persisted inbound replay state.
falsification_cases.gate: cargo run --quiet --manifest-path scripts/hegemon_formal_core/Cargo.toml -- check-blueprint config/formal-security-blueprint.json --claims config/formal-security-claims.json

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks protocol replay-state duplicate rejection. Persisted native bridge replay reload is covered by native.bridge-replay-reload. This node does not prove BLAKE3 replay-key derivation, bridge light-client validity, bridge receipt soundness, or full native-node equivalence beyond the node's use of the protocol-kernel replay helper.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean native tx-leaf artifact parser, valid bounded artifact bytes are accepted with the expected summary, missing backend bytes default according to the tx version/default backend rule, and malformed backend IDs, trailing bytes, over-cap counts, over-cap proof lengths, over-cap commitment rows/coefficients, and truncation are rejected.
nodes.informal_argument: Native tx-leaf artifacts are a proof-system trust boundary before consensus consumes receipt metadata. The Lean parser models the exact byte grammar and count caps used by the production decoder. `gen_native_tx_leaf_artifact_vectors` emits accepted and rejected artifacts, and the superneo-hegemon conformance test checks production decoding and canonical re-encoding against those Lean-derived cases.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for native tx-leaf artifact parser grammar and canonical re-encoding behavior.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the native tx-leaf artifact parser theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing production native tx-leaf artifact decoding or canonical re-encoding away from the Lean executable parser must fail the generated-vector conformance step.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks native tx-leaf artifact byte grammar, count caps, backend-byte defaulting, trailing-byte rejection, and canonical re-encoding behavior only. It does not prove lattice backend security, STARK proof soundness, receipt-root fold soundness, statement-digest collision resistance, or full native backend cryptographic acceptance.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean native receipt-root parser and structural gate, valid bounded artifact bytes parse with the expected summary, non-empty expected leaf sets must match artifact leaf count, fold_count must equal leaf_count - 1, each fold must carry exactly five challenges and exact 11 by 54 parent-row shape, and malformed counts, trailing bytes, and truncation are rejected.
nodes.informal_argument: Native receipt-root artifacts are consumed after tx-leaf verification and before backend fold verification. The Lean parser models the bounded byte grammar, and the Lean schedule predicate models the cheap production validator now called by both native receipt-root verifier entry points before cryptographic fold checks. `gen_native_receipt_root_vectors` emits accepted parser/schedule cases and rejected mismatch cases, and the superneo-hegemon conformance test checks production decoding, canonical re-encoding, and structural-gate agreement.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for native receipt-root parser grammar and structural fold-schedule gating. Scope excludes cryptographic fold soundness.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the native receipt-root parser/schedule theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing production native receipt-root decoding, canonical re-encoding, or structural scheduling away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks native receipt-root byte grammar and cheap structural fold-schedule gating only. It does not prove lattice backend security, backend transcript challenge derivation, fold proof soundness, statement-digest collision resistance, or full native backend cryptographic acceptance.

nodes.formal_statement: The executable Lean native-backend review policy accepts a vector bundle only when the backend remains candidate-under-review / structural-candidate, claimed security metadata is not below the configured floor, case names are unique, every case kind is supported, positive cases omit expected-error evidence, negative cases include expected-error evidence, and all required tx-leaf and receipt-root valid/invalid cases are present. The independent reference verifier must then accept the committed valid artifacts and reject malformed companions for spec digest, parameter fingerprint, proof digest, STARK proof, fold-row, root-commitment, and trailing-byte failures.
nodes.informal_argument: Native proof artifacts are a trust boundary. The Lean theorem `Hegemon.Native.NativeBackendReviewPolicy.accepts_iff_native_backend_review_preconditions` pins the review policy, companion theorems pin posture, security-floor, duplicate-name, unsupported-kind, malformed-expectation, and missing-case rejection precedence, generated vectors are checked by `scripts/check_native_backend_review_policy_vectors.py`, and `native-backend-ref` remains the byte-level independent verifier for the actual bundle.

target_review.notes: The target is accepted as a vector and byte-grammar claim, not as a cryptographic proof of the candidate backend.

falsification_cases.description: The vector bundle includes invalid cases that must continue to be rejected by the reference verifier.

Lean-generated native-backend review-policy vectors must fail on unsupported posture, insufficient security-floor metadata, duplicate case names, unsupported kinds, malformed expected-error flags, and missing required cases.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks review-policy coverage for the native backend vector bundle and then runs the independent byte verifier. It does not certify the lattice backend's security assumptions, the Module-SIS reduction, backend transcript soundness, BLAKE3 collision resistance, or full proof-system soundness.

nodes.formal_statement: The executable release RISC Zero verifier predicate accepts exactly when image id, journal decoding, and verifier-enabled preconditions all hold; the shipped release configuration sets verifier-enabled false, so no release receipt can be accepted after prechecks.
nodes.informal_argument: RISC Zero bridge receipts remain offline experimental artifacts because the verifier path can pull classical Groth16/BN254 code into the binary. The Lean theorem `Hegemon.Native.Risc0ReleaseVerifier.release_build_never_accepts` proves the release-disabled predicate is fail-closed, companion theorems pin image/journal rejection precedence, and generated Lean vectors are checked against the production helper used by `verify_risc0_bridge_receipt`. The existing node regression still checks that message-binding tampering rejects before the disabled verifier path and that a bound RISC Zero receipt is not staged.

target_review.notes: Accepted as a fail-closed residual-risk node; it is not production-eligible bridge receipt acceptance.

falsification_cases.description: Inbound bridge message-binding tampering must be rejected by node tests.
Lean-generated release-verifier vectors must match the production RISC Zero release-verifier helper.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks the fail-closed release decision table only. It explicitly does not permit inbound RISC Zero receipt acceptance on the release node and does not prove future PQ bridge receipt soundness.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean native action stream, acceptance determines the same planned commitment-start sequence and next leaf cursor accepted by the canonical action-plan application model, and an accepted consensus tree transition exposes the applied commitment-tree root.
nodes.informal_argument: `Hegemon.Native.CommitmentTreeRefinement.accepted_stream_commitment_starts_from` proves that accepted action-stream replay determines a commitment-start schedule. `commitment_starts_plan_application_accepts` and `accepted_stream_canonical_plan_accepts` prove that replaying those counts and starts through the action-plan admission model accepts with the same next leaf cursor. `accepted_stream_tree_transition_refines_applied_root` then composes that plan fact with `Hegemon.Consensus.TreeTransition.accepted_transition_returns_applied_root` so an accepted tree transition publishes the applied root.

target_review.notes: Accepted as a non-production implementation-equivalence refinement across existing Lean action-stream, action-plan, and tree-transition kernels. It does not add a new Rust conformance vector or claim complete commitment-tree integrity.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the native commitment-tree refinement theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
The release formal-core wrapper must include the native commitment-tree refinement module in the Lean root and claims ledger.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves a Lean refinement boundary between existing action-stream planning, action-plan application, and consensus tree-transition models. It does not prove Merkle hash security, note commitment correctness, transaction proof soundness, production Rust tree implementation equivalence, reorg/startup replay equivalence, storage durability, or complete native-node refinement.

nodes.formal_statement: The executable Lean native-backend release-posture policy accepts the default release package exactly when review_state is candidate-under-review, maturity_label is structural-candidate, and external cryptanalysis is not marked complete; accepted release mode accepts only with review_state accepted, no known incomplete external-review marker, and a checked-in acceptance artifact that states external acceptance.
nodes.informal_argument: The repo still does not claim machine-checked cryptographic soundness for the native lattice backend. The Lean theorem `Hegemon.Native.NativeBackendReleasePosture.accepts_iff_release_posture_preconditions` pins the release-posture decision table, companion theorems pin candidate and accepted acceptance/rejection cases, generated vectors are checked by `scripts/check_native_backend_release_posture_policy_vectors.py`, and the existing shell gate still evaluates the packaged review posture used by release workflows.

target_review.notes: Accepted as an explicit residual-risk node with theorem-backed release-posture gating. This does not promote the native backend to production cryptography.

falsification_cases.description: Lean-generated native-backend release-posture vectors must fail on wrong candidate posture, completed external-review marker in candidate mode, wrong accepted posture, incomplete accepted external review, missing accepted artifact, and malformed accepted artifact text.
The native backend posture script must report candidate-under-review and structural-candidate for the current package.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node does not claim the native backend is production-ready cryptography.
